/*!
    @page Flexio_I2s_Ip_example_S32K344_group FLEXIO I2S IP Transfer
    @brief Example application showing FLEXIO I2S IP usage

    ## Application description ##
    _____
    The purpose of this demo application is to show the usage of the FLEXIO I2S driver found
    on the S32K3XX SoC FLEXIO I2S IP.

    The application uses FLEXIO I2S IP driver to make a data transfer of a defined size.
    The application contains 2 Flexio channels according to Master and Slave Channels, and it is divided into 2 parts
        - Part 1: Master sends data, Slave receives data
        - Part 2: Slave sends data, Master receives data
    To check if data is transfered correctly, put break point or let program run for about 2 seconds, then see if RecvData content is the same as SendData.

    How the example works:
    - If all conditions are correct, the code goes to the end of program without stuck in loop and bStatus as TRUE

    ## Prerequisites ##
    _____
    To run the example you will need to have the following items:
    - S32K3XX board
    - 1 Power adapter for the board
    - PnE Debugger

    ## Boards supported ##
    _____
    The following boards are supported by this application:
    - S32K3X4EVB-Q257 47827 PCB RevX1 SCH RevA. Silicon: P32K344EHVMMS 0P55A CTSK2038A
    - S32K3X4EVB-Q172 51972 PCB RevA SCH RevA. Silicon: P32K344EHVPBS 1P55A CTZX2128B

    ## Hardware Wiring ##
    _____
    - The debugger must be connected to JTAG Cortex Debug connector J52 10-pin with S32K3X4EVB-Q257 or J12 20-pin with S32K3X4EVB-Q172

    PIN FUNCTION                              | S32K3X4EVB-Q257   | S32K3X4EVB-Q172   |
    ------------------------------------------|-------------------| ------------------|
    FlexioD16 Slave TX - FlexioD12 Master RX  | P3.13 - P3.29     | J37.12 - J38.6    |
    Flexio19 Slave RX - FlexioD10 Master TX   | P3.11 - P3.25     | J37.10 - J3.11    |
    FlexioD18 Slave SCK - FlexioD7 Master SCK | P3.9  - P3.23     | J37.8  - J3.30    |
    FlexioD3 Slave WS - FlexioD4 Master WS    | P3.37 - P3.39     | J38.18 - J38.20   |

    ## How to run ##
    ______
    #### 1. Importing the project into the workspace ####
    After opening S32 Design Studio, go to \b File -> \b New \b S32DS \b Project \b From... and select \b Flexio_I2s_Ip_example_S32K344. Then click on \b Finish. \n
    The project should now be copied into you current workspace.
    #### 2. Generating the S32 configuration ####
    The example will run without an active configuration, however if any changes are required, a configuration needs to be generated.
    The initial configuration will have the same settings as the default example settings.
    First go to \b Project \b Explorer View in S32 DS and select the current project(\b Flexio_I2s_Ip_example_S32K344).
    Select the "S32 Configuration Tool" menu then click on the desired configuration tool (Pins, Clocks, Peripherals etc...). Clicking on any one of those will generate all the components.
    Make the desired changes(if any) then click on the "S32 Configuration Tool->Update Code" button.
    #### 3. Building the project ####
    Select the configuration to be built \b RAM (Debug_RAM) or FLASH (Debug_FLASH) by left clicking on the downward arrow corresponding to the \b build button(@image hammer.png).
    Wait for the build action to be completed before continuing to the next step.
    #### 4. Running the project ####
    Go to \b Run and select \b Debug \b Configurations. There will be a debug configuration for this project:
     Configuration Name                               | Description
     -------------------------------------------------|------------
     \b Flexio_I2s_Ip_example_S32K344_Debug_RAM_PNE   | Debug the RAM configuration using GDB PEMicro
     \b Flexio_I2s_Ip_example_S32K344_Debug_FLASH_PNE | Debug the FLASH configuration using GDB PEMicro

    \n Select the desired debug configuration and click on \b Launch. Now the perspective will change to the \b Debug \b Perspective. \n
    Use the controls to control the program flow.

    @note For more detailed information related to S32 Design Studio usage please consult the available documentation.
*/
